q:
A magnet, when moved near a coil, produces an induced current. Which of the following method(s) can be used to increase the magnitude of the induced current?
(i) Increasing the number of turns in the coil
(ii) Increasing the speed of the magnet
(iii) Increasing the resistivity of the wire of coil
Which team won the Santosh Trophy 2021-22, the 75th edition of the Football tournament?
The Asiatic lion population largely resides in the protected park area of __________.
The fraction equivalent to 0.474747… is:
The rate of increase in consumption due to a unit increment in income is called __________.

Which of the following numbers will replace the question mark (?) in the given series?
13, 21, 33, 54, 91, ?

Three statements are given, followed by two conclusions numbered I and II. Assuming the statements to be true, even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements.
Statements:
1. Some avalanches are precipitations.
2. Some catastrophes are avalanches.
3. All avalanches are storms.
Conclusion:
I. Some storms are catastrophes.
II. No precipitation is storms.
o: Neither conclusion I nor II follows
Both conclusion I and II follow

q: In a certain code language, TRIP is written as WULS. How will CLAN be written in the same language?
A and B invest ₹47,000 and ₹56,000 respectively, in a business. At the end of the year they make a profit of ₹87,220. Find B's share in the profit.
What is the angle traced by the hour hand in 23 minutes?

Which of the following options is the closest approximate value which will come in place of question mark (?) in the following equation?
49.9 × 3.99 ÷ 2.97 + 17.88 = ?

Three statements are followed by three conclusions numbered I, II and III. You have to consider these statements to be true, even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts, and decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the given statements.
Statements:
All toys are plastics.
All plastics are disposables.
Some toys are filters.
Conclusions:
(I) Some filters are plastics.
(II) Some toys are filters.
(III) Some disposables are filters.
o: Only conclusions I and II follow
Either conclusion I or conclusion III follows
Only conclusions II and III follow

q: All the compounds of which of the following sets belong to the same homologous series?

o: either a convex lens or a concave mirror
either concave lens or a convex mirror
either a convex lens or a convex mirror
either a concave lens or a concave mirror

q: Mendeleev's Periodic Law states that:
o: the properties of elements are the periodic function of their colour
the properties of elements are the periodic function of their atomic numbers
the properties of elements are the periodic function of their magnetic properties
the properties of elements are the periodic function of their atomic masses

q: Who is the Chief Election Commissioner of India as on March 2022?

2, 14, 70, 210, ?

Three of the following statements pertaining to non-biodegradable plastics indicate their implications on animals, plants and our surroundings. Choose the odd one out.
o: Reduce the fertility of the soil
Plastic bags are durable carry bags
Can prove fatal when eaten by animals

q: A class of 50 students appeared in a test. The average score of 12 students is 80, and that of the rest is 75. What is the average score of the class?

Identify the FALSE statement.
o: The sunflower is a phototrophic plant.
The growth of pollen tubes towards ovules is an example of geotropism.
Geotropism is demonstrated by the downward migration of roots.
Hydrotropism is the tendency to grow in moist areas, i.e., the movement of roots towards high humidity.

q:
Given below are three statements and three conclusions. Take the statements to be true even if they are at variance with commonly known facts, and decide whether the conclusions follow the given statement.
Statements:
I. Some trees are bushes.
II. Some bushes are creepers.
III. Some creepers are stems.
Conclusions:
I. Some trees are trees.
II. Some creepers are trees.
III. Some stems are bushes.

A statement is given, followed by four conclusions given in the options. Find out which conclusion is true based on the given statement.
Statement: K = U > N > S > E > G > W
Find the roots of 6/x − 2/(x−1) − 1/(x−2) = 0.

L, K, J, M, G, F, D and S live on eight different floors of the same building. The lowermost floor in the building is numbered 1, the floor above it, number 2 and so on till the topmost floor is numbered 8. L lives exactly one floor above K and exactly one floor below G. Only K lives between D and G. J lives between M and L. Only D lives on the 8th floor, while L lives on the 5th floor. On which floor does K live?

Two wires A and B are made of same material and have the same length but different cross-sectional areas. If the resistance of wire A is 9 times the resistance of wire B, the ratio of the radii of wire A to that of wire B is:
If x² + 2x + 9 = (x − 2)(x − 3), then the resultant equation is:

o: the physical conditions of the atmosphere keep changing
the atmosphere consists of a mixture of gases
the physical conditions of the atmosphere are stationary

o: Dr. Amit Singh, Dr. Kanishka Biswas and Dr. Anish Ghosh

q: How many seconds will a boy take to run one complete round around a square field of side 16 metres, if he runs at a speed of 4 km/h?

(A) The mirror is concave.
(B) The mirror forms a virtual image.
(C) The mirror has positive focal length.
______ is represented by the root apex's constantly dividing cells.

This question is based on the five, four-digit numbers given below:
(Left) 4178   6235   3746   2255   5675 (Right)
(Example: 6972 = First digit = 6, second digit = 9, third digit = 7 and fourth digit = 2)
(NOTE - All operations to be done from left to right.)
If 3 is added to the first digit and 2 is subtracted from the last digit of every number, what will be the product of the third digit of the largest number and the second digit of the smallest number?

Based on the given statement, two conclusions are drawn. Find out which conclusion is true based on the statement.
Statement: H ≥ M = G, S < G ≥ Y = L
Conclusions:
I. H > Y
II. M < L

SMILE : ELIMS :: MASTE : ETSAM :: STARV : ?

A vendor sold 25 laptops at a profit of 32% and 15 laptops at a profit of 20%. If he had sold all the 40 laptops at a profit of 18%, his profit would have been increased by ₹30,000. What is the cost price of each laptop, if the cost price of all the 40 laptops is the same?

Read the given statement and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow/s from the statement.
Statement:
Cyclone Sagar is expected to make landfall by 3 p.m. today on the Baleness coast. Heavy rain and strong winds are expected to lash Baleness from 11 a.m. onwards. Power supply in Baleness is likely to be affected from 2 p.m. to 8 p.m.
Conclusions:
I. There could be power cuts in Baleness after 3 p.m. today.
II. It would be unsafe for fishermen to venture out into the sea today, till the cyclone passes.
o: Neither conclusion I nor II follows
Both conclusions I and II follow

q:
Q started from a point and walked towards the north for 42 m, then from there he turned right and walked 7 m, then he turned right again and walked 30 m, and then turned left and walked 10 m. In which direction is Q facing now?
(All turns are 90 degree turns only)

Study the given letter series and answer the question that follows.
K S O N P R F U E T B A C E F M N
How many such vowels are there each of which is immediately followed by atleast two consonants appearing together in the above series?

Which of the following alphanumeric clusters will replace the question mark (?) in the series to make it logically complete?
BE 2, GF 3, LG 5, Q7 7, ?

Vikas took a bus from a bus stop. The bus travelled 50 m towards the east. Then, it took a right turn and travelled 70 m, then it took a left turn and travelled 15 m. Then, it took a left turn and travelled 50 m. Then, it took a right turn and travelled 10 m. Finally, it took a left turn and travelled 30 m to reach the next stop. How far and in which direction is the next stop from where the bus started? (All turns are 90 degree turns only)

o: decreasing cross sectional area of the conductor
increasing cross sectional area of the conductor

q:
G, H, I, J, K and L live on six different floors of the same building. The lowermost floor in the building is numbered 4, the floor above it is numbered 5, and so on till the topmost floor is numbered 1.
J lives on an even numbered floor. G and K each live on an odd numbered floor. I lives on floor number 2. H lives on the floor immediately above I. G does not live on the topmost floor. Who lives on the lowermost floor?
Which of the following medicinal plants is the best remedy to treat blood pressure?
Which of the following is the greatest number that divides 72 and 119 and leaves 3 and 4 as respective remainders?
